messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: Express
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: Express, Styling: Tailwind CSS — create hello world app where I can modify the text \"hello world\" colors through a frontend UI",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "init-express-project",
      "title": "Initialize Express project",
      "description": "Create a new Node project, initialize package.json, install Express and basic dev dependencies, and set up a Git repo.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A project folder with package.json, Express installed, and a basic server entry file (e.g., server.js or app.js) that can be started with npm run dev or node."
    },
    {
      "id": "configure-tailwind",
      "title": "Install and configure Tailwind CSS",
      "description": "Install Tailwind and required build tools, create tailwind.config.js, add Tailwind directives to a CSS entry file, and configure build pipeline (PostCSS or Tailwind CLI).",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-express-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Tailwind is integrated into the frontend build so that utility classes are available and a sample Tailwind class (e.g., bg-red-500) affects the served HTML."
    },
    {
      "id": "create-static-server-and-build-scripts",
      "title": "Serve static frontend and add npm scripts",
      "description": "Configure Express to serve a static directory (e.g., /public), add npm scripts for dev and build (start, dev, build-css), and ensure built assets are served correctly.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "init-express-project"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Express serves files from /public and npm scripts run to start the server and build Tailwind CSS without errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "build-frontend-html-and-ui",
      "title": "Create index.html with Hello World and UI controls",
      "description": "Add an index.html in the static folder using Tailwind classes that displays the 'hello world' text and includes UI controls (color picker(s) or select inputs) for modifying the text color.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "configure-tailwind",
        "create-static-server-and-build-scripts"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "index.html loads in the browser, shows the Hello World text, and includes visible UI controls for choosing colors."
    },
    {
      "id": "implement-color-change-logic",
      "title": "Implement client-side color change functionality",
      "description": "Add JavaScript to listen to the UI controls and update the Hello World text color in real time; optionally save selection to localStorage so color persists across reloads.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "build-frontend-html-and-ui"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Changing the UI controls immediately updates the text color in the page and the chosen color persists on reload if localStorage is implemented."
    },
    {
      "id": "add-api-endpoint-for-persistence",
      "title": "Optional: Add Express API to persist color server-side",
      "description": "Create simple GET/POST endpoints in Express to save and retrieve the selected color; update client code to fetch initial color and post changes when user updates color.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-color-change-logic",
        "create-static-server-and-build-scripts"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Server API can store and return a color value and the frontend reads the saved color on load and sends updates when changed."
    },
    {
      "id": "test-and-document",
      "title": "Test functionality and write README",
      "description": "Manually test all flows (start server, change color, persistence), fix issues, and write a README with setup, run, and usage instructions including example commands.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "implement-color-change-logic",
        "add-api-endpoint-for-persistence"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "All manual tests pass, server runs, UI updates colors, persistence works (local or server), and README contains clear setup and usage steps."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Functional decomposition: split the project into backend setup, styling configuration, static serving, frontend UI creation, behavior implementation, optional server-side persistence, and final testing/documentation."
}
